parsed.mechanism: Conda's preconditioned optimization geometry induces faster convergence, and transplanting that principle into MaTTS regulates how scaled agent interactions are weighted, causing diverse experiences to converge into higher-quality synthesized memory in fewer interaction rounds.
parsed.transfer: The convergence-acceleration mechanism from A transfers to B as a memory-synthesis schedule that produces high-quality memory from fewer scaled interactions.
parsed.open_problem: Can a Conda-style preconditioned update rule applied to memory synthesis cut the number of agent interactions needed for MaTTS to reach a target memory quality by 2x?
parsed.niche_name: Convergence-Accelerated Memory Synthesis for Test-Time Scaling
parsed.primary_quote: scales up agent interactions to generate diverse experiences
